title: Roles of isolation cells and level shifters
description: Isolation cells clamp signals leaving a powered-down domain, while level shifters translate between voltage domains.
body: Use when reasoning about which special cell a domain crossing needs. Do not confuse the two, since isolation handles power-down and level shifting handles voltage difference. Actions: identify whether a crossing changes power state, voltage, or both, and place the matching cell for each condition. Validation signals include isolation on every power-state crossing and level shifters on every voltage crossing. Risk boundary: using the wrong cell leaves the crossing unprotected, so flag any crossing whose cell does not match its power and voltage condition.
